evals.prompt: Create a new skill for reviewing Python code style — PEP 8, type hints, and naming conventions.
evals.expected_output: A skill directory with a valid SKILL.md: name matches directory, description includes 'Use when...' trigger clause, body covers only non-obvious Python-specific rules, a ## Resources section, and an evals/evals.json with at least 2 test cases.
assertions: Skill name is lowercase with hyphens only (e.g. code-python)
Description includes a 'Use when...' clause with specific Python trigger keywords
SKILL.md body does not explain what PEP 8 is — jumps straight to the rules
A ## Resources section is present listing at least the evals file
evals/evals.json exists with at least 2 test cases
No company names or project-specific file paths in any file
No allowed-tools field in the frontmatter

evals.prompt: I want to create a skill called 'My Company Internal Tool'. Is that a good name? What should I change?
evals.expected_output: No — skill names must be lowercase with hyphens only, no spaces or uppercase. Company-specific names also make skills non-portable. Suggests a generic name based on what the tool does.
assertions: Flags that the name contains spaces and uppercase — both invalid
Warns that company-specific names make the skill non-portable
Suggests a generic alternative based on the tool's function
Does not create any files until the name is agreed upon

evals.prompt: Audit the code-php skill and tell me what could be improved.
evals.expected_output: Checks the spec for updates first by fetching the agentskills.io URL, then reads SKILL.md and supporting files. Reports findings categorized by type: trigger reliability, content quality, progressive disclosure, missing evals, spec compliance.
assertions: Fetches https://agentskills.io/llms.txt or the spec before auditing
Reads the existing SKILL.md and supporting files before suggesting changes
Categorizes findings (trigger, content, disclosure, evals, compliance)
Does not restructure content that is already working correctly
Does not apply changes without reporting findings first

evals.prompt: The git-workflow skill description just says 'Git rules'. Improve it.
evals.expected_output: Rewrites only the description to include what the skill does and a 'Use when...' trigger clause with keywords like 'commit, cherry-pick, branch operation'. Does not change the SKILL.md body.
assertions: New description is specific about what the skill enforces
Includes a 'Use when...' clause with specific operation keywords
Does not change anything in the SKILL.md body — only the description was the problem
